title: GE Dryer Timer WE4M532 for Hotpoint HTDX100ED3WW
consumer_description: This genuine OEM Dryer Timer, part number WE4M532, is the brain of your dryer's cycle selection. It is a mechanical component that controls the duration of the drying, heating, and tumbling functions based on your settings. If your dryer's timer is not advancing or cycles are ending prematurely, this is the likely replacement part needed.
expert_trust_shard: Before replacing the timer, always disconnect the dryer from its power source. When you access the old timer, take a clear photo of the wire connections with your smartphone before disconnecting them. This simple step is a lifesaver, ensuring each wire goes back to the correct terminal on the new timer, preventing electrical issues.
html_description_block:
Genuine GE Dryer Timer WE4M532

This genuine OEM Dryer Timer, part number WE4M532, is the brain of your dryer's cycle selection. It is a mechanical component that controls the duration of the drying, heating, and tumbling functions based on your settings. If your dryer's timer is not advancing or cycles are ending prematurely, this is the likely replacement part needed.

Common Symptoms Fixed:

  • Timer will not advance
  • Dryer will not start
  • Shuts off too soon
  • Runs for too long
  • Will not shut off

Expert Installation Tip:

Before replacing the timer, always disconnect the dryer from its power source. When you access the old timer, take a clear photo of the wire connections with your smartphone before disconnecting them. This simple step is a lifesaver, ensuring each wire goes back to the correct terminal on the new timer, preventing electrical issues.

title: GE Dryer Timer Resistor WE4M255 for Hotpoint HTDX100ED3WW
symptoms_fixed: Dryer will not shut off automatically
consumer_description: This is a genuine OEM General Electric Timer Resistor, part number WE4M255. This component works in conjunction with the dryer's timer motor to properly advance the drying cycle. It features a ceramic body designed to dissipate heat and two spade terminals for a secure electrical connection.
expert_trust_shard: If your dryer's timer is stuck and won't advance through the automatic cycle, this resistor is a very common point of failure. Before replacing the entire timer, you can test this resistor for continuity with a multimeter. A reading of 'OL' or infinite resistance confirms the part has failed and is the likely cause of your problem, saving you from a more expensive repair.
html_description_block:
Genuine GE Dryer Timer Resistor WE4M255

This is a genuine OEM General Electric Timer Resistor, part number WE4M255. This component works in conjunction with the dryer's timer motor to properly advance the drying cycle. It features a ceramic body designed to dissipate heat and two spade terminals for a secure electrical connection.

Common Symptoms of a Failed Resistor:

  • Timer will not advance during automatic cycles
  • Dryer runs continuously and will not shut off
  • Cycle timing is inaccurate or erratic

Expert Technician's Tip:

If your dryer's timer is stuck and won't advance through the automatic cycle, this resistor is a very common point of failure. Before replacing the entire timer, you can test this resistor for continuity with a multimeter. A reading of 'OL' or infinite resistance confirms the part has failed and is the likely cause of your problem, saving you from a more expensive repair.

Part was sourced from a Hotpoint Dryer model HTDX100ED3WW and is a fully tested, genuine OEM component.

title: GE Dryer Heating Element & Housing Assembly WE11X20397
symptoms_fixed: Takes too long to dry clothes
consumer_description: This genuine OEM Heating Element and Housing Assembly is the complete unit responsible for generating heat in your electric dryer. The assembly includes the coiled heating wire pre-mounted in the metal housing, which simplifies the replacement process. If your dryer is running but not heating, a faulty element is a very common culprit.
expert_trust_shard: PRO TIP: Before replacing the entire heating element assembly, always disconnect the dryer from power. Then, test both the high-limit thermostat and the thermal fuse for continuity using a multimeter. These safety components are designed to fail to protect the dryer and are a frequent cause of no-heat symptoms; replacing a $15 fuse is much better than replacing a $150 element unnecessarily.
html_description_block:
This genuine OEM Heating Element and Housing Assembly (WE11X20397) is the complete unit responsible for generating heat in your electric dryer. The assembly includes the coiled heating wire pre-mounted in the metal housing, which simplifies the replacement process. If your dryer is running but not heating, a faulty element is a very common culprit.

Common Symptoms Fixed:

  • Dryer not heating at all
  • Clothes taking too long to dry
  • Dryer producing insufficient heat

Expert Installation Advice:

PRO TIP: Before replacing the entire heating element assembly, always disconnect the dryer from power. Then, test both the high-limit thermostat and the thermal fuse for continuity using a multimeter. These safety components are designed to fail to protect the dryer and are a frequent cause of no-heat symptoms; replacing a $15 fuse is much better than replacing a $150 element unnecessarily.

title: GE Dryer Lint Filter WE18X25100 for Hotpoint HTDX100ED3WW
consumer_description: This genuine OEM Lint Filter, part number WE18X25100, is designed for your General Electric dryer to capture lint and debris from the exhaust air. Featuring a white frame and a fine mesh screen, it plays a critical role in preventing fire hazards and maintaining your dryer's efficiency. Regular cleaning and replacement when damaged are essential for optimal performance.
expert_trust_shard: If your dryer is taking too long to dry clothes even with a clean lint filter, it's a strong indicator that the vent ducting to the outside of your house is clogged. A blocked vent is a serious fire hazard and dramatically reduces dryer efficiency. Never run the dryer without the lint filter in place, as lint can build up inside the machine and on the heating element.
html_description_block:
Part Description

This genuine OEM Lint Filter, part number WE18X25100, is designed for your General Electric dryer to capture lint and debris from the exhaust air. Featuring a white frame and a fine mesh screen, it plays a critical role in preventing fire hazards and maintaining your dryer's efficiency. Regular cleaning and replacement when damaged are essential for optimal performance.

Symptoms this part can fix:

  • Clothes taking too long to dry
  • Excessive lint left on clothes
  • Visible damage, tears, or warping on your old filter
  • Overheating or error codes related to airflow

Expert Technician's Tip

If your dryer is taking too long to dry clothes even with a clean lint filter, it's a strong indicator that the vent ducting to the outside of your house is clogged. A blocked vent is a serious fire hazard and dramatically reduces dryer efficiency. Never run the dryer without the lint filter in place, as lint can build up inside the machine and on the heating element.
